questions.question: What is the main purpose of Gemini Notebook?
choices: To browse the open web and pull in context from any source automatically
To work exclusively from sources you provide, synthesizing them without outside context
To create video content from your webcam and edit it automatically
To replace all other Google productivity tools in your workflow
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly—Gemini Notebook is grounded in your material only, which keeps the output trustworthy and traceable.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Gemini Notebook actually works only from sources you give it, not from the open web. This is a feature, not a limitation, because it means everything it generates is grounded in your actual material.

questions.question: What is Studio in Gemini Notebook?
choices: A video recording studio where you film yourself presenting
The panel inside every notebook where you generate rich content formats like Video Overviews
A premium subscription tier that costs extra money
A collection of templates you can use to design presentations
questions.feedback_correct: Right—Studio is where you turn your source material into structured outputs like Video Overviews and Audio Overviews.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Studio is not a physical space or a paid upgrade. It's the content generation panel built into every notebook where you create outputs from your sources.

questions.question: What is a Video Overview in Gemini Notebook?
choices: A casual, unstructured recording of you talking about a topic
A structured, presenter-style summary built directly from your source material, like a briefing
A compilation of video clips from YouTube related to your topic
An interactive quiz that tests your knowledge of a source
questions.feedback_correct: That's correct—a Video Overview is designed to get someone up to speed fast, much like a briefing before a meeting.
questions.feedback_incorrect: A Video Overview is structured and generated from your sources, not casual or pulled from the web. It's designed to be a professional summary that synthesizes your material.

questions.question: What should you do immediately after adding a website URL as a source?
choices: Start typing questions in the search box right away
Click Generate to create a Video Overview immediately
Watch the Sources panel on the left until the source appears with a title and summary
Navigate away and come back to it after 24 hours
questions.feedback_correct: Perfect—watching the Sources panel confirms that Gemini Notebook has processed your material and is ready to use.
questions.feedback_incorrect: You need to wait for processing to complete first. When the source appears in the list with a title and summary, that's your confirmation that the material is indexed and ready to work with.

questions.question: In what order do the core steps of creating a Video Overview occur?
choices: Open Gemini Notebook, create a notebook, add a source, open Studio, generate the Video Overview
Add a source, create a notebook, open Studio, open Gemini Notebook, generate the Video Overview
Open Studio, create a notebook, open Gemini Notebook, add a source, generate the Video Overview
Generate the Video Overview, create a notebook, add a source, open Studio, open Gemini Notebook
questions.feedback_correct: Excellent—you have the sequence down: sign in, create the workspace, add your material, open the generation tool, then create the output.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The correct order starts with opening Gemini Notebook and signing in, then creating a named notebook, adding your website source, opening Studio, and finally generating the Video Overview. This progression ensures your workspace and material are ready before you generate content.

questions.question: Why is it important that Gemini Notebook does not perform open web searches when generating content?
choices: Because it saves money on API costs and makes the tool run faster
Because it ensures all output is grounded in your material and traceable, not hallucinated
Because the tool is not allowed to access the internet for security reasons
Because it prevents the tool from competing with Google Search
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly—working only from your sources means every insight in the output can be traced back to something you actually provided, which makes the tool trustworthy.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The reason Gemini Notebook avoids open web searches is to ground the output in your actual material. This prevents hallucination and makes every result verifiable, which is a core strength of the tool.

questions.question: After you complete the hands-on exercise, what resource does the course offer to help you remember the steps?
choices: A video recording of an instructor demonstrating the entire process again
A certification badge you can display on your LinkedIn profile
A written, step-by-step document you can keep and reuse
Access to a live chat support group with other learners
questions.feedback_correct: That's right—you can ask for a written summary of the steps you completed, which you can keep as a reusable reference.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The course provides a written document you can request that captures every step you just completed. You can use this as a reference guide whenever you want to repeat the process with new material.

questions.question: Which of the following is the best example of when Gemini Notebook would add genuine value to your actual work?
choices: Brainstorming creative ideas for a marketing campaign with no reference material
Loading a PDF report you need to understand before a meeting and generating a Video Overview to watch instead
Replacing all of your note-taking apps with a single tool
Creating social media content without reviewing any sources first
questions.feedback_correct: Perfect—this is exactly the kind of practical use case where Gemini Notebook shines: taking real material you have and synthesizing it into a consumable format.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Gemini Notebook works best when you bring your own material to it. The value comes from loading actual documents, reports, or pages you need to understand and generating structured summaries from them, not from working without sources.

questions.question: What should happen if you encounter an issue or uncertainty while completing the hands-on steps?
choices: Skip the step and move on to the next one without help
Close Gemini Notebook and start over from the beginning
Take your time with each step and refer back to the instructor notes for that slide when needed
Wait until the course is completely finished before asking any questions
questions.feedback_correct: That's the right approach—the instructor notes on each slide are there to guide you through every step at your own pace.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Each slide has detailed instructor notes that walk you through exactly what to do and what to expect. If you're uncertain about a step, review those notes on that slide—they're designed to support you through the entire hands-on exercise.
